Second: maybe Catholic hierarchy vs other Christian: Archbishop, Cardinal, Nun, Monk (Catholic), vs others (Reverend, Minister, Pastor). Could ask about being a high-ranking Catholic official.

Q5: Is the target word commonly used in Protestant denominations?" Reverend, Minister, Pastor all used. Hmm.

We need differentiate three.

Perhaps ask about being addressed as "Your Honor"? That's for Cardinal/Archbishop.

Maybe ask about using the title "Reverend" before the name - that is Reverend not others.

Or ask: "Is the title commonly preceded by 'The Reverend'?" That's only Reverend.

So Q5: "Is the title commonly preceded by the word 'The' (as in 'The Reverend')?" If yes -> Reverend.

If no -> remaining Minister, Pastor.

Now we have two.

Q6: "Is the title commonly used to refer to a church leader in a Baptist congregation?" That would be Pastor. Minister can be more generic.

If yes -> Pastor.

If no -> Minister.

Then guess at Q7? Actually we have used up to Q6; we still have one more question to confirm maybe.

But we must guess after max 7 questions, and have only one guess. So we can guess after Q6 if we have identified.

Thus total questions max 6. Good.

Let's execute.
